system_description.overview: Doe is an AI-powered platform for dental groups that automates procurement management, provides operational intelligence, and optimizes supply chain and vendor relationships. The system ingests dental group data to surface actionable insights and automate ordering workflows.

products.Automated Procurement Management: AI platform for dental groups automating procurement workflows
products.AI-Powered Operational Intelligence: Analytics and insights layer for dental operations
products.Supply Chain and Vendor Optimization: Vendor relationship and supply chain management module

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out — AWS is a subservice organization)
regions: Not explicitly named; single region implied

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with network ACLs; production environment isolated
network_architecture.firewalls: AWS security groups and network ACLs
network_architecture.topology: Internet → Route 53 → ALB → ECS Fargate (backend); AWS Amplify (frontend); Secrets Manager, S3, CloudWatch, WAF, GuardDuty, IAM Identity Center, AWS Backup all within VPC boundary

application_architecture.environments: Separate environments (production isolated); specifics not detailed

databases: Not explicitly named beyond AWS managed services

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for admin/privileged accounts and SCM access
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Enforced via AWS IAM Identity Center

ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Merge to main requires approval; branch protection rules enforced
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool with baseline configs as code
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Patch management policy; regular patching cycle

security_tools.antivirus: Antivirus installed on all servers and desktops; weekly AV definition updates

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — physical access controls delegated to AWS (subservice)
A1.2 — environmental protections delegated to AWS (subservice)
Processing Integrity criteria — not part of engagement scope
Privacy criteria — not part of engagement scope
notable_controls: COSO principles and CC1-CC9 trust services criteria tested
Carve-out method applied for AWS as subservice organization
Separate Availability trust criteria (A1.1, A1.3) included

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ on AWS with WAF, GuardDuty, ECS Fargate, IaC baseline configs. Single region and no CDN or multi-region failover limit the score.

application_architecture.rationale: ECS Fargate containerization and branch protection are positives. No named languages/frameworks beyond Amplify frontend; no mention of automated testing.

data_layer.rationale: S3 and AWS Backup with daily backups and KMS encryption. No named database type, no caching layer, no data warehouse disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: Strong posture: WAF + GuardDuty IDS + monthly vuln scans + annual external pen test + antivirus + KMS + MFA. Missing SIEM and cyber insurance disclosure.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC (baseline configs), branch protection, container orchestration (ECS), separate environments. CI/CD tool unnamed; no explicit automated testing pipeline mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, annual review and testing confirmed, daily backups, multi-AZ failover. No stated RTO/RPO and no multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavily concentrated on AWS with no named secondary cloud or significant third-party services beyond AWS ecosystem. Single critical dependency risk.

overall.rationale: Solid AWS-native SOC 2 Type 1 with good security controls and multi-AZ. Weaknesses are single-region, single-vendor concentration, undisclosed tech stack specifics, and no stated RTO/RPO.

red_flags: No RTO/RPO disclosed — availability commitments unquantified
Single cloud provider (AWS) with no secondary cloud — concentration risk
yellow_flags: CI/CD tool unnamed — pipeline maturity unclear
Database technology not named — data layer opacity
SOC 2 Type 1 only (point-in-time) — no operational evidence period
green_flags: Multi-AZ failover with daily AWS Backup
Quarterly access reviews with 1-business-day revocation SLA
MFA enforced on admin accounts and SCM
Unqualified audit opinion with zero exceptions
key_observations: Dental-industry vertical AI platform — PHI/HIPAA adjacency likely even if not in SOC 2 scope
AWS Amplify for frontend suggests modern managed hosting approach
Carve-out method for AWS means AWS physical/environmental controls not independently tested — reliance on AWS SOC 2 report required
ACCORP Partners template-based report — same structure as other ACCORP clients in this batch
